meta.title: Visual Composition Rules for SOTY Event Horizon
meta.author: Cleanlystudio
meta.description: Comprehensive composition database including classical rules, cinematic framing, and WebGL-specific techniques

rule_of_thirds.description: Divide the frame into a 3x3 grid. Place key elements along grid lines or at intersection points (power points).

power_points.strength: Strong — primary focus in Western left-to-right reading
Strong — secondary focus, natural eye movement end
Moderate — terminal position for eye movement

web_design: Place the black hole center slightly off-center (rule of thirds position) for dynamic tension
Align text blocks to the left or right third
Place key interactive elements at power points
Horizon lines (if any) along horizontal thirds, not center
when_to_break: Centered composition creates symmetry and stability — intentional for 'singularity' moments
Full-bleed elements that fill the entire viewport (immersive scenes)
When the subject IS the center (the black hole itself)

golden_ratio.description: Found throughout nature (nautilus shells, sunflower seeds, galaxy spirals). Creates compositions that feel naturally balanced.

layout_proportions.description: Divide layout widths/heights by φ for balanced proportions

spacing.description: Use φ to create harmonious spacing scales

typography.description: Font sizes based on golden ratio

event_horizon_specific.black_hole_placement: The center of the black hole at φ intersection creates more dynamic composition than dead center
event_horizon_specific.accretion_disk_proportions: Inner ring radius to outer ring radius should approximate 1:φ
event_horizon_specific.text_placement: Narrative text positioned at φ distance from the focal black hole

golden_spiral.description: A logarithmic spiral with growth factor of φ. Guides the eye from the largest element to the smallest along a natural curve.
golden_spiral.construction: Based on golden rectangles: subdivide a golden rectangle and draw quarter-circle arcs through each subdivision

general: Place the most important element (black hole) at the spiral's focal point (smallest rectangle)
Arrange secondary elements along the spiral path — the eye follows naturally
Use the spiral as a guide for particle flow and light trails
event_horizon_specific: The accretion disk naturally follows a spiral — align visual elements to golden spiral overlay
Text blocks can be positioned along the spiral's outer curve
Light bending (gravitational lensing) follows spiral-like paths around the black hole
The spiral termination point = the singularity = the void = maximum emotional impact

fibonacci_sequence.description: Each number is the sum of the two preceding. Ratio between consecutive numbers approaches φ.
design_applications: Particle distribution patterns (sunflower algorithm for even distribution)
Sound frequency ratios for harmonic relationships

visual_hierarchy.description: The order in which the human eye perceives elements. Establish clear hierarchy so users process information in the intended order.

size.principle: Larger elements are perceived as more important
size.implementation: Use type scale ratios. Hero text 4-6x body size.
size.effectiveness: Highest — size is the strongest hierarchy signal
size.example: Chapter title at 90px, body text at 16px — 5.6x ratio

color_and_contrast.principle: High-contrast and saturated colors draw attention first
color_and_contrast.implementation: Key elements in high contrast (white/cyan on black). Secondary in lower contrast (gray on dark gray).
color_and_contrast.effectiveness: Very high — especially on dark backgrounds where light elements pop

position.principle: Elements at the top and left are seen first (Western reading). Center for focused layouts.
position.implementation: Critical info top-left or center. Supporting info below or in periphery.
position.example: Chapter number top-left, title center, description below

whitespace.principle: Elements with more surrounding space feel more important (they 'breathe')
whitespace.implementation: Give key elements generous padding. Pack secondary items tighter.
whitespace.effectiveness: High — whitespace signals luxury and importance
whitespace.example: Chapter title with 20vh top padding vs data readouts packed in a HUD corner

motion.principle: Moving elements attract attention before static ones
motion.implementation: Animate the element you want users to notice first. Static for secondary.
motion.effectiveness: Highest immediate attention but can be distracting if overused
motion.example: Text reveal animation draws attention to narrative while particles orbit passively

depth_and_blur.principle: Sharp elements feel closer and more important; blurred elements recede
depth_and_blur.implementation: Depth of field effect — foreground sharp, background soft
depth_and_blur.effectiveness: Medium — works best in combination with other techniques
depth_and_blur.example: Text in focus, background particles slightly blurred

texture_and_detail.principle: Detailed/textured elements attract more attention than flat ones
texture_and_detail.implementation: Use grain, noise, or shimmer on key elements. Clean/flat for background.
texture_and_detail.effectiveness: Low-medium — subtle but contributes to premium feel

hierarchy_order_for_event_horizon: 1. The black hole visualization (always dominant — it IS the experience)
2. Chapter title text (dramatic reveal, large size)
6. Background star field and particles (ambient, atmospheric)

focal_point_placement.description: The single most important element in the composition where the eye should land first.
techniques_to_create_focal_point: Contrast: the brightest or most saturated element in the frame
Convergence: leading lines pointing toward it
Detail: most detailed element in a simple scene
Motion: the only moving element (or the stillest in a moving scene)
Anomaly: the one element that breaks the pattern

placement_strategies.effect: Maximum stability, authority, confrontation. The viewer faces it directly.
Tension, anticipation, the unknown beyond the frame.
placement_strategies.when: Before the black hole is revealed, building suspense

event_horizon_focal_progression.focal: Centered starfield → eye drawn to brightest star
Black hole at rule-of-thirds position → approaching
Black hole moves to center → confrontation
Void → dispersal → new focal point emerges

leading_lines.description: Visual elements that guide the viewer's eye along a path toward the focal point.

types.name: Explicit lines
types.examples: Particle trails, accretion disk streaks, HUD grid lines

types.name: Implied lines
types.examples: Alignment of scattered elements, text alignment, eye gaze direction

types.name: Curved lines
types.examples: Gravitational lensing arcs, orbital paths, spiral arms

types.name: Converging lines
types.examples: Perspective lines converging at black hole, light rays bending inward

types.name: Diagonal lines
types.examples: Particle streams at angles, slanted text reveal

event_horizon_applications: Particle streams flowing toward the black hole = leading lines to the singularity
Gravitational lensing creates curved light paths that circle the focal point
Accretion disk acts as a massive circular leading line around the center
HUD data readout lines can converge toward the data source
Star streaks during approach sequence lead toward the destination

types.name: Bilateral
types.description: Mirror image across a vertical axis

types.name: Radial
types.description: Elements radiate from a center point

symmetry.psychological_effect: Humans are wired to find symmetry beautiful (facial symmetry preference). Creates feelings of balance, order, completeness.
symmetry.event_horizon_use: The black hole IS radially symmetric. This natural symmetry creates a hypnotic, almost spiritual visual. Kubrick used bilateral symmetry for HAL 9000 and the Stargate.

asymmetry.description: Balanced but not mirrored. Creates dynamic tension and visual interest.
techniques: Balance large element on one side with smaller detailed elements on the other
Use color intensity to balance visual weight across asymmetric layout
Text blocks on one side balanced by visual elements on the other
asymmetry.psychological_effect: More dynamic and engaging than symmetry. Feels modern, active, alive.
asymmetry.event_horizon_use: Text positioned asymmetrically against the centered black hole creates tension between order (the void) and chaos (human narrative)

negative_space.description: The empty space around and between elements. Not 'nothing' — it's an active design element.
principles: Negative space defines the shape of positive space — they are inseparable
More negative space = more premium, luxurious, contemplative feel
Less negative space = more dense, urgent, information-rich feel
In dark themes, negative space IS the void — it has psychological weight
Active negative space: intentionally shaped to create meaning (FedEx arrow)
application_for_event_horizon: The void of space IS the negative space — it's not decorative, it's the experience
Allow the blackness to breathe. Don't fill every pixel with particles or effects
Text floating in vast darkness creates intimacy (small human words in infinite void)
Chapter transitions should use PURE negative space (blackout moments) for emotional reset
The black hole's shadow is the ultimate negative space — absence as the main character

ratios.event_horizon_target: 75-85% negative space during narrative, 40-60% during data displays

depth_cues.description: Visual techniques that create the illusion of three-dimensional depth on a 2D screen.

monocular_cues.name: Relative size
monocular_cues.implementation: Particles further from camera rendered smaller. Stars in background are pinpoints.

monocular_cues.name: Overlap (occlusion)
monocular_cues.description: Objects in front partially hide objects behind
monocular_cues.implementation: Accretion disk occluding stars behind it. Text floating in front of particles.

monocular_cues.name: Atmospheric perspective
monocular_cues.description: Distant objects are desaturated, lower contrast, and blueshift
monocular_cues.implementation: Far stars: blue-shifted, low opacity. Near elements: full saturation and contrast.
monocular_cues.strength: Strong — creates vast sense of distance

monocular_cues.name: Blur (depth of field)
monocular_cues.description: Objects at the focus distance are sharp; others are blurred
monocular_cues.implementation: Post-processing bokeh/DOF effect in the shader pipeline
monocular_cues.strength: Strong — mimics how the human eye works

monocular_cues.name: Linear perspective
monocular_cues.description: Parallel lines converge at a vanishing point
monocular_cues.implementation: Grid lines, light rays converging toward the black hole
monocular_cues.strength: Very strong in scenes with straight lines

monocular_cues.name: Texture gradient
monocular_cues.description: Texture becomes finer and denser with distance
monocular_cues.implementation: Star field density increases toward the horizon. Particle detail decreases with distance.

monocular_cues.name: Motion parallax
monocular_cues.description: Near objects move faster across the view than far objects
monocular_cues.implementation: Multi-layer parallax in star field. Near particles orbit faster than distant ones.

monocular_cues.name: Height in picture plane
monocular_cues.description: Objects higher in the frame appear farther away
monocular_cues.implementation: Less applicable in space scenes (no ground plane) but useful for HUD layering

depth_stacking_for_event_horizon.name: Background stars
depth_stacking_for_event_horizon.characteristics: Pinpoint white, no motion, lowest opacity

depth_stacking_for_event_horizon.name: Nebula/dust clouds
depth_stacking_for_event_horizon.characteristics: Soft, low opacity, subtle color, slow drift

f_pattern.description: Users scan the top left, move right, drop down, scan left to right again (shorter), continue down the left edge
f_pattern.relevance_to_event_horizon: Low — F-pattern applies to text-heavy pages, not immersive experiences

z_pattern.description: Eye moves from top-left → top-right → diagonal to bottom-left → bottom-right
z_pattern.application: Simple pages with limited content, landing pages
z_pattern.relevance_to_event_horizon: Medium — useful for chapter title layouts with minimal elements

gutenberg_diagram.description: The page is divided into quadrants. Primary optical area (top-left), strong fallow area (top-right), weak fallow area (bottom-left), terminal area (bottom-right). Eye naturally flows from primary to terminal.

center_focus_pattern.description: In immersive/cinematic experiences, the eye is drawn to the center first, then explores outward
center_focus_pattern.relevance_to_event_horizon: HIGH — the black hole at center draws all attention. Everything else is explored from center outward.
center_focus_pattern.design_implication: Place the most important visual (black hole) at or near center. Position text where the eye naturally moves after center (left or right sides, below center).

proximity.description: Elements close to each other are perceived as a group
proximity.application: Group related data points in the HUD. Related text blocks should be closer than unrelated ones.
proximity.spacing_rule: Space within groups < space between groups. Ratio should be at least 1:2.

similarity.description: Elements that look similar are perceived as related
similarity.application: Use consistent styling for all HUD data elements. Same font for all narrative text. Same color for all interactive elements.
similarity.examples: All clickable elements in cyan. All chapter titles in Space Grotesk Bold. All measurements in JetBrains Mono.

closure.description: The mind fills in missing information to perceive complete shapes
closure.application: The black hole's event horizon can be implied by the accretion disk — you don't need to draw the actual edge. Partial UI frames suggest complete structures.
closure.examples: HUD corner brackets suggest a complete frame without drawing all edges. Partial circle of particles implies the full orbit.

continuity.description: The eye follows smooth paths and lines, even through intersections
continuity.application: Particle trails should flow smoothly. Scroll animations should have continuous motion. Transitions should carry visual momentum.
continuity.examples: Accretion disk material flows in smooth orbits. Text reveal follows a consistent direction.

figure_ground.description: The eye separates elements into figure (foreground subject) and ground (background)
figure_ground.application: Ensure clear separation between the black hole (figure) and space (ground). Text (figure) must be clearly distinguishable from the WebGL canvas (ground).
figure_ground.challenges: In dark themes, figure-ground separation relies heavily on luminance contrast since color cues are limited.

common_fate.description: Elements moving in the same direction are perceived as a group
common_fate.application: Particles orbiting the same direction form a unified disk. Staggered text animation where all text moves the same direction feels cohesive.

pragnanz.description: The mind prefers the simplest interpretation of visual information
pragnanz.application: Keep compositions clean. Remove any element that doesn't serve a purpose. The black hole's circular form is already the simplest — don't overcomplicate it.

twelve_column_grid.description: The standard responsive grid — 12 columns divide evenly by 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 12
twelve_column_grid.application: Layout of text blocks, HUD panels, and UI elements overlaying the WebGL canvas
twelve_column_grid.event_horizon_adaptation: Use 12-column grid for text overlay positioning but allow the WebGL scene to break the grid freely

modular_grid.description: Both columns and rows create a matrix of modules
modular_grid.module_sizes: Based on base unit (typically 8px or line-height). Modules are multiples of the base unit.

baseline_grid.description: All text sits on a consistent baseline rhythm — typically the body line-height
baseline_grid.application: Ensures vertical rhythm across all text elements
baseline_grid.implementation: If body line-height is 24px, all spacing values should be multiples of 24 (or 12 for half-line)

full_screen_composition_grid.description: For immersive full-viewport experiences, use a flexible composition grid

zones.name: Safe area
zones.description: Content must stay within this area for all screen sizes

kubrick_symmetry.description: Stanley Kubrick's signature one-point perspective with bilateral symmetry
kubrick_symmetry.effect: Creates unease, perfection, artificial order, the sublime
kubrick_symmetry.application: Center the black hole perfectly. Use bilateral symmetry for the most powerful, unsettling moments. The Stargate sequence from 2001 is the direct ancestor of Event Horizon's visual language.
kubrick_symmetry.technique: Camera (viewport) aimed directly at the vanishing point. All elements mirrored left-right.

villeneuve_sci_fi_framing.description: Denis Villeneuve's approach to sci-fi — vast scale with human smallness
villeneuve_sci_fi_framing.application: Make the black hole MASSIVE relative to any human-scale elements. Use extreme scale contrast — tiny text against infinite void. Let emptiness tell the story.
villeneuve_sci_fi_framing.technique: Extreme wide shots. Subject occupies < 10% of frame. Vast empty space creates meaning.

nolan_time_distortion.description: Christopher Nolan's use of time manipulation as a visual and narrative device
nolan_time_distortion.effect: Disorientation, urgency, the malleability of reality
nolan_time_distortion.application: Time dilation near the black hole: slow-motion effects, time readouts changing speed, sound pitch-shifting. Interstellar's Gargantua is the most direct reference.
nolan_time_distortion.technique: Cross-cutting between different time rates. Audio pitch-shifting. Clock-like sounds decelerating.

wes_anderson_palette.description: Wes Anderson's precise, curated color palettes and centered framing
wes_anderson_palette.application: NOT directly applicable to Event Horizon's dark aesthetic, but the PRINCIPLE of a precisely controlled, limited palette is essential. Every color must be intentional.
wes_anderson_palette.technique: Limit palette to 4-6 colors. Every color justified. No accidental colors.

ridley_scott_atmosphere.description: Ridley Scott's use of atmospheric perspective, smoke, and light shafts
ridley_scott_atmosphere.application: Particle fog, volumetric light through the accretion disk, atmospheric haze around the black hole. Light passing through matter creates depth and mood.
ridley_scott_atmosphere.technique: Layered atmosphere — foreground haze, midground detail, background glow. Visible light beams.

full_screen_webgl_composition.description: Specific rules for composing within a full-viewport WebGL canvas

principles.name: Respect the viewport aspect ratio
principles.description: Different screens have wildly different aspects (16:9, 16:10, 4:3, ultrawide 21:9, mobile 9:16). Composition must work across all.
principles.solution: Use viewport-relative positioning (percentages). Anchor key elements to viewport center or edges, not absolute positions.

principles.name: The center is the anchor
principles.description: In full-screen immersive experiences, the center is the natural focus point. Everything is positioned relative to center.
principles.solution: Place the black hole at or near center. Text and HUD elements positioned relative to center (offset in viewport units).

principles.name: Edge awareness
principles.description: Content near viewport edges may be cut off on smaller screens or obscured by browser chrome.
principles.solution: Keep critical content within a 90% safe area (5% inset from each edge). Decorative elements can bleed beyond.

principles.name: Scroll-driven composition changes
principles.description: As the user scrolls, the composition should evolve — zooming, panning, revealing new elements
principles.solution: Map scroll position to composition parameters. At scroll 0%: wide establishing shot. At scroll 50%: close-up. At scroll 100%: inside the void.

principles.name: Layer text over predictable zones
principles.description: Text must always be readable. Position text over areas of the scene that remain consistently dark/uniform.
principles.solution: Define 'text-safe zones' in the WebGL scene where the shader guarantees low luminance and no bright particles.

composition_during_scroll.description: How visual composition should evolve as the user scrolls through the experience

phases.elements: Star field, subtle nebula, empty space
Black hole silhouette visible, first particles, first text
Full accretion disk, active particles, detailed data, narrative text
phases.focal_point: Black hole growing, moving toward center

phases.elements: Close-up black hole, intense accretion, gravitational distortion, time dilation effects
Spaghettification effects, reality distortion, color shifts, visual breakdown
New visual state — either void, rebirth, or static

radial_composition.description: Specific to the black hole — composition organized around a central void with orbital elements
principles: The void IS the composition. Everything else orbits around absence.
Concentric rings create natural visual hierarchy (center = most important)
Radial symmetry is hypnotic — use intentional asymmetric breaks to maintain interest
Elements closer to center: smaller, faster, hotter (orange/white)
Elements farther from center: larger, slower, cooler (blue/purple)
The event horizon boundary is the strongest compositional line — the divide between visible and invisible
